text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 59-year-old female with history of FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, BRCA1 germline mutation. Status post primary debulking surgery (R0) followed by adjuvant Paraplatin/pacli x6 cycles. Currently on nira maintenance therapy (approximately 15 months). Surveillance restaging.

COMPARISON: Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (approximately 9 months prior).

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of IV contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL). Oral contrast was not administered. Images were acquired in the portal venous phase with multiplanar reconstructions.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:
The lungs are clear bilaterally. No focal consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pleural-based nodularity. No pulmonary nodules identified. Minimal dependent atelectasis at the lung bases bilaterally, unchanged. The pleural spaces are clear without effusion. No pneumothorax. The pericardium is unremarkable. The mediastinum is normal in contour. No hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy. The visualized thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. Osseous structures of the thorax are intact without suspicious lytic or blastic lesions.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous in attenuation without focal lesion. No hepatic mass, cyst, or hypodense/hyperdense lesion identified. The hepatic contour is smooth. Intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated.

Gallbladder: Absent, consistent with prior cholecystectomy (surgical clips noted in the gallbladder fossa). No biliary ductal dilatation. The common bile duct measures approximately 5 mm, within normal limits in the setting of prior cholecystectomy.

Spleen: Absent, consistent with prior splenectomy at time of primary debulking. Post-splenectomy changes present without complication.

Pancreas: The pancreas appears unremarkable in signal and contour. No ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid collection.

Kidneys/Adrenals: The kidneys are symmetric in size and enhancement pattern. No hydronephrosis. No focal renal mass or perinephric collection. Small bilateral renal cortical cysts, unchanged. The adrenal glands are normal in morphology bilaterally.

Bowel: The small bowel loops are not dilated. No bowel wall thickening or pneumatosis. The colon appears unremarkable in caliber without mass lesion. No free air.

Vascular: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. No aneurysmal dilatation. Celiac axis, SMA, and SMV appear patent. No thrombosis identified in the portal or mesenteric venous system.

Peritoneum/Retroperitoneum: No ascites. No peritoneal thickening, nodularity, or implants. The mesentery is fatty and homogeneous without evidence of infiltration or nodularity. The retroperitoneal fat planes are preserved. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. Para-aortic and paracaval nodal stations are unremarkable. The largest visualized node in the para-aortic region measures approximately 7 mm in short axis (stable compared with prior).

PELVIS:
Post-surgical changes are noted consistent with prior pelvic debulking including total hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. Surgical clips are present throughout the pelvis. The pelvic floor soft tissues appear stable without nodular soft tissue recurrence. No discrete pelvic mass. No pelvic sidewall lymphadenopathy. The iliac nodal chains are unremarkable bilaterally. The bladder appears decompressed and within normal limits. No pelvic free fluid. No enhancing soft tissue implants along the pelvic peritoneum or cul-de-sac.

BONE WINDOWS: No suspicious osseous lesion. No lytic or sclerotic lesion to suggest metastatic involvement.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of disease recurrence. The prior complete radiographic response is maintained. No peritoneal implants, ascites, or lymphadenopathy to suggest recurrent ovarian carcinoma.

2. Post-surgical changes as above, including absent spleen and gallbladder, and pelvic debulking changes — all stable and unremarkable.

3. Stable small subcentimeter para-aortic lymph node, likely reactive. No interval change.

4. No new thoracic, abdominal, or pelvic findings of concern.

Electronically signed by: [Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging]
Dictated and finalized.
